steps.title: Find the total painted area
steps.content: Painting the walls twice means covering a total area of $2w$ square feet, because the original wall area is counted once for each coat.

steps.title: Use the coverage rate
steps.content: At 1 gallon per 70 square feet, the gallons needed are $P = \frac{2w}{70}$.

steps.title: Simplify and match
steps.content:
Simplify: $\frac{2w}{70} = \frac{w}{35}$. This keeps the units as gallons and accounts for both coats.

That matches Choice D — $P=\frac{w}{35}$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $P=\frac{w}{70}$: This covers the walls only once, not twice. ✗
  • Choice B — $P=70w$: This multiplies instead of divides, giving a nonsensically large number of gallons. ✗
  • Choice C — $P=140w$: Also multiplies, making $P$ grow with $w$ at a rate of 140. Dimensionally wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — $P=\frac{w}{35}$: Equivalent to $\frac{2w}{70}$, correctly accounting for two coats. ✓
That matches Choice D — $P=\frac{w}{35}$.
